channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A long-lost ancient Roman artifact reappears in a New Orleans backyard
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A New Orleans family cleaning up their overgrown backyard made an extremely unusual find: Under the weeds was a mysterious marble tablet with Latin characters that included the phrase “spirits of the dead.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A look at how Venezuelans in the US are reacting to Maria Corina Machado's Nobel Prize win
item.description: Venezuelans in the U.S. are welcoming the news that opposition leader leader Maria Corina Machado won the Nobel Peace Price.
item.dc:creator: Gisela Salomon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Blast at a Tennessee explosives plant leaves 18 missing and feared dead, sheriff says
item.description: McEWEN, Tenn. (AP) — A blast leveled an explosives plant Friday in rural Tennessee, leaving behind a mass of twisted metal, burned-out shells of cars and at least 18 people missing and feared dead, authorities said. “There’s nothing to describe.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Pentagon will build a training facility for Qatari pilots in Idaho
item.description: The U.S. military is moving forward with plans to build a dedicated facility in Idaho to train pilots from Qatar, an important U.S. ally in the Middle East, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: President Donald Trump is in 'exceptional health,' his doctor says, after visit to Walter Reed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is in “exceptional health," his physician said Friday after he underwent a checkup that included lab tests and preventive health assessments at Walter Reed National Military Medical Center.
item.dc:creator: SEUNG MIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: OSHA violations reported prior to massive explosion at Tennessee facility: documents
item.description: As investigators continue to comb through the wreckage of an explosion in Tennessee that left 18 people unaccounted for Friday, Nexstar's WKRN has learned more about the company that operated the facility.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Wilson, KREX 5

item.title: Nor'easter without a name to bring ocean flooding and high winds to US East Coast
item.description: A storm without a name was expected to bring flooding to the East Coast from South Carolina to New Jersey and high winds to New York City over the holiday weekend. While the storm affecting the eastern U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WGN employee detained amid ICE activity in Chicago, later released
item.description: An employee of WGN-TV was detained by two Border Patrol officers and later released after federal agents swarmed a busy intersection in Chicago Friday Morning.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, Fox 5

item.title: National Guard stands down in Illinois while troops patrol in Memphis with local police
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — National Guard troops patrolled in Tennessee's second-largest city for the first time Friday while soldiers in Illinois were engaged only in planning and training after a judge handed a loss to the Trump administration and blocked their deployment to the Chicago area.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump threatens tech export limits, new 100% tariff on Chinese imports starting Nov. 1 or sooner
item.description: Trump said that “starting November 1st, 2025 (or sooner, depending on any further actions or changes taken by China), the United States of America will impose a Tariff of 100% on China, over and above any Tariff that they are currently paying.
item.dc:creator: KGET 17, NBC

item.title: TGI Fridays offering free food for some federal workers amid shutdown
item.description: The promotion will be available all day, every day during the two-week period, unless the shutdown ends before Oct. 23.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Sam’s Club is changing its Sunday hours: What to know
item.description: Sam’s Club shoppers will soon have extended access to their preferred pallets of toilet paper and sacks of oranges.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Arctic seals, more than half of bird species on latest threatened list
item.description: While many animals are increasingly at risk of disappearing forever, the updated list shows how species can come back from the brink with dedicated effort.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sister Jean Dolores Schmidt, beloved chaplain at Loyola University Chicago, dies at 106
item.description: Sister Jean Dolores Schmidt, a beloved member of the Loyola University Chicago community who was well-known as the chaplain to the Loyola University men's basketball team, died Thursday at the age of 106, according to the university.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, Fox 5

item.title: Most states planning to withdraw National Guard troops from DC this fall
item.description: Nearly half the roughly 2,450 National Guard troops deployed to Washington, D.C., amid President Donald Trump's crackdown on crime are reportedly set to leave the city later this fall.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Air traffic controllers who duck unpaid work during the gov't shutdown could be fired, Duffy warns
item.description: Even though the U.S. has a stark shortage of air traffic controllers, ones who call in sick instead of working without a paycheck during the federal government shutdown risk being fired, the U.S. transportation secretary warned.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Did Trump end 7 wars? What to know ahead of Nobel Peace Prize announcement
item.description: President Trump's moment of truth as a global peacemaker will come early Friday morning when the Nobel Peace Prize is awarded. Trump has made no secret of his desire to win the prize.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DHS blames Democrats for shutdown in video to be shown at TSA checkpoints
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is rolling out a new video of DHS Secretary Kristi Noem blaming Democrats for the government shutdown,
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Blast at a Tennessee explosives plant leaves 18 missing and feared dead, sheriff says
item.description: Officials say an explosion at a Tennessee explosives plant has left 18 people missing and feared dead.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Fear and vigilance rise as attacks on houses of worship intensify worldwide
item.description: Every week hundreds of millions of people around the world gather to worship in peace. But for some, there comes a day when deadly violence invades their sacred spaces and shatters that sense of sanctuary and safety. It happened recently at a synagogue in England and two churches in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, PETER SMITH and TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alex Jones asks Supreme Court to halt $1.4 billion defamation judgement
item.description: Conspiracy theorist Alex Jones urged the Supreme Court on Thursday to immediately halt a $1.44 billion defamation judgment he owes for falsely claiming the Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting was a hoax.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KRON4

item.title: GOP senators worry Trump is undercutting their shutdown strategy
item.description: Senate Republicans are grumbling that President Donald Trump undercut their strategy for ending the government shutdown when he said that he’s willing to cut “the right deal” with Democrats on health care.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: Opposition activist María Corina Machado of Venezuela wins the Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: The former opposition presidential candidate in Venezuela was lauded for being a “key, unifying figure in a political opposition that was once deeply divided — an opposition that found common ground in the demand for free elections and representative government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Pope urges news agencies to stand as bulwark against lies, manipulation and post-truths
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Leo XIV encouraged international news agencies on Thursday to stand firm as a bulwark against the “ancient art of lying” and manipulation, as he strongly backed a free, independent and objective press.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge blocks National Guard deployment in Illinois for 2 weeks
item.description: A judge on Thursday blocked the deployment of National Guard troops in the Chicago area for at least two weeks, finding no substantial evidence that a “danger of rebellion” is brewing in Illinois during Trump's immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO and SUDHIN THANAWALA, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Tennessee teacher fired over Charlie Kirk posts files free speech lawsuit
item.description: A longtime Rutherford County teacher is suing her school district, claiming she was fired for what she shared online.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Milan, ABC 4

item.title: Saying 'I do' to AI? Ohio lawmaker proposes ban on marriage, legal personhood for AI
item.description: “As the computer systems improve in their capacity to act more like humans, we want to be sure we have prohibitions in our law that prohibit those systems from ever being human in their agency.
item.dc:creator: Ava Boldizar, NewsNation

item.title: Drake's defamation lawsuit against label over Kendrick Lamar's 'Not Like Us' tossed out
item.description: In his lawsuit Drake asserted that the song amounts to “falsely accusing him of being a sex offender, engaging in pedophilic acts” and more.
item.dc:creator: Larry Newumeister, KRON4

item.title: Arizona sheriff's office misused millions meant to remedy racial profiling, report reveals
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — The sheriff’s office for metro Phoenix spent millions of dollars budgeted for compliance costs in a racial profiling case over Joe Arpaio’s immigration crackdowns on things that had little or nothing to do with a court-ordered overhaul of the agency, according to an expert’s report.
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alex Jones asks Supreme Court to halt $1.4 billion defamation judgment
item.description: Conspiracy theorist Alex Jones urged the Supreme Court to immediately halt a $1.44 billion defamation judgment he owes for falsely claiming the Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting was a hoax.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: ICE agents clash with cemetery workers attempting to help man in river
item.description: It is currently unknown whether the cemetery workers involved will face charges in connection with the incident on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Julian Crews, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kiss rocker Gene Simmons recovering after crashing SUV
item.description: MALIBU, Calif. (AP) — Kiss rock star Gene Simmons is recovering after he fainted while driving along the Pacific Coast Highway in Malibu and crashed his SUV into a parked car, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Flight delayed or canceled during government shutdown? Here's what you should do
item.description: The federal government shutdown has entered its second week, and already shortages of air traffic controllers have strained operations and disrupted flights at some U.S. airports.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK and RIO YAMAT AP Transportation and Airlines Writers, Fox 5

item.title: October Prime Day is over, but these deals are still live
item.description: Amazon’s 48-hour mega sale is back, with exclusive deals for Prime members across nearly every category.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Luna, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Democrats win momentum over GOP in shutdown fight
item.description: As the shutdown fight stretches into its second week, the winds have shifted in favor of the Democrats — at least for the moment. Early polls say voters are more likely to blame President Trump and the Republicans for the lengthy impasse.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Israel, Hamas agree to 'first phase' of plan to end fighting, release hostages, Trump says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Israel and Hamas have agreed to the “first phase” of his peace plan to pause fighting and release at least some hostages and prisoners, U.S. President Donald Trump said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Woman sues haunted house after breaking both ankles
item.description: A Nebraska woman says a jump meant to thrill visitors at a haunted house in Kansas City, Missouri, left her with two broken ankles.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, Fox 5
